problem_title: Company data checks remain disconnected and manual
problem_description: Compliance teams struggle to verify counterparty registry information quickly due to fragmented sources and manual data consolidation. This slows onboarding decisions and weakens audit trails needed for defensible business relationship approvals.
problem_point_1_value: KYC, KYB, KYT and Travel Rule outputs sit in separate tools
problem_point_2_value: Teams move data between dashboards, spreadsheets and internal notes
problem_point_3_value: Regulatory changes are hard to map into internal workflows
problem_point_4_value: Evidence and decision history are difficult to reconstruct later
solution_description: SFTY integrates disparate company registry checks, KYC and KYB outputs into one structured onboarding workflow that supports risk-based routing, decision tracking and audit-ready evidence for compliance teams.
workflow_title: From counterparty registry checks to onboarding approval
workflow_description: SFTY organizes registry data, screening results and provider outputs into risk-based reviews, cases and an audit trail to support onboarding decisions.
workflow_step_1_description: KYC, KYB, KYT and screening outputs enter SFTY
workflow_step_2_description: Risk rules route checks into the right workflow
workflow_step_3_description: A case opens with customer, wallet and provider context
workflow_step_4_description: Teams review, decide and record the outcome
workflow_step_5_description: Logs, decisions and reports stay ready for audit

why_sfty_card_3_description: Preserve evidence, reviewer actions and decision history
why_sfty_card_4_description: Give compliance, risk and leadership one operating view

ask_sfty_helper_text: AI assistant for product, deployment and compliance questions
faq_description: Clear answers on provider orchestration, Travel Rule workflows, audit evidence and enterprise compliance operations.
faq_1_question: What is SFTY for counterparty registry checks?
faq_1_answer: SFTY provides a compliance layer that connects company registry data with KYC and KYB outputs to support structured, auditable counterparty verification workflows for VASPs and regulated businesses.
faq_2_question: How does SFTY structure counterparty onboarding?
faq_2_answer: SFTY orchestrates checks from multiple providers, applies internal policies, routes reviews, and creates cases to support consistent counterparty onboarding with risk-based decision and audit trails.
faq_3_question: How does SFTY preserve onboarding audit evidence?
faq_3_answer: SFTY preserves onboarding case history, provider outputs, decision logs and evidence packages to maintain an auditable trail that supports regulatory review and internal compliance checks.
faq_4_question: Can SFTY connect existing KYC and KYB providers?
faq_4_answer: Yes. SFTY integrates with existing KYC, KYB and registry providers, connecting their results into connected onboarding workflows without requiring replacement of these tools.
faq_5_question: Does SFTY replace registry or KYC providers?
faq_5_answer: No. SFTY does not replace KYC, KYB or registry providers but acts as a control layer that structures and manages their outputs into compliance-ready workflows and evidence.
final_title: Structure counterparty registry reviews with SFTY
final_description: Review how SFTY connects company registry data and KYB outputs into structured onboarding workflows and audit-ready evidence.
footer_description: Supervisory and compliance control layer for digital asset markets, businesses and regulated institutions.
